baseNote: Rates show ZMW per 1 unit of foreign currency. ↑ ZMW = Kwacha weakening (depreciating).
mpRateNote: Monetary Policy Rate held at 13.50% — reduced 75bps from 14.25% on Feb 11, 2026. BOZ held rate at May 2026 review. Next review: August 2026.

USD.note: Jul 6: 1 USD ≈ 18.35 ZMW mid-market (XE). Kwacha slightly softer from 18.30 (Jul 2) and weaker week-on-week from 18.04 (Jun 25). Market consolidating in 18.00–18.50 range.

GBP.note: 1 GBP ≈ 24.57 ZMW (Jul 6). Sterling slightly firmer from 24.28 (Jul 2). Up 3.4% week-on-week.

EUR.note: 1 EUR ≈ 20.99 ZMW (Jul 6). Euro slightly up from 20.82 (Jul 2). Up 2.4% week-on-week.

ZAR.note: 1 ZAR ≈ 1.13 ZMW (Jul 6). Rand slightly stronger from 1.12 (Jul 2). 1 ZMW ≈ 0.885 ZAR.

BWP.note: Botswana pula. 1 BWP ≈ 1.283 ZMW. Stable cross-rate.

CNY.note: Chinese yuan. 1 CNY ≈ 2.67 ZMW. Kwacha weakening slightly against yuan.

trendAnalysis.direction: Kwacha slightly softer — mid-market at 18.35 (XE Jul 6) vs 18.25 (Jul 2). Kwacha weakening week-on-week from 18.04 (Jun 25). EM currencies under pressure from broad USD strength ahead of US election expectations.
drivers: Record copper output 890K tonnes (2025) — strong export earnings
Record maize harvest >4M tonnes — reduced food import needs
Debt restructuring ~94% complete — sovereign risk premium collapsing
IMF successor programme talks scheduled July 2026 — staff visit confirmed
MPR at 13.5% — high real yields attracting carry trade inflows
Yuan mining tax corridor reducing USD demand
Gross international reserves at historic high of US$6.5 billion
ERB cut fuel prices for July — lower import bill supporting kwacha
Pre-election spending starting to add pressure on kwacha
trendAnalysis.outlook: Kwacha consolidating in 18.00–18.50 range. BOZ likely to hold MPR at August review. Pre-election spending adding modest pressure. Fuel price cut provides offset.
trendAnalysis.projection3m: USD/ZMW 16.50–18.50 range. Elections expected to drive volatility.
trendAnalysis.projection6m: USD/ZMW 16.00–19.00 range. Post-election clarity determines direction.
